rules.rule: Always use read_file to get exact content including whitespace before calling edit_file, never reconstruct old_text from memory
rules.match_pattern: old_text not found.*whitespace differs|first line matches but whitespace
rules.fix_hint: Re-read the file with read_file and copy the exact text verbatim, preserving all indentation and spacing

rules.rule: Run rustfmt or cargo fmt after writing Rust code to ensure formatting matches project conventions
rules.fix_hint: Run cargo fmt on the file or crate before verifying changes

rules.rule: Run Flutter/Dart commands from the directory containing pubspec.yaml (the project root)
rules.fix_hint: cd to the project root directory containing pubspec.yaml before running Flutter/Dart commands

rules.rule: Before running Flutter widget tests, verify that test files reference constructors that exist in the codebase (e.g., update MyApp references if the main app widget was renamed)
rules.match_pattern: Couldn't find constructor|Failed to load.*test.*\.dart|Compilation failed.*test
rules.fix_hint: Check the test file for constructor references (like MyApp) and update them to match the actual class/constructor names in lib/

rules.rule: Before referencing a module in Rust code, ensure it is declared with `mod` in the crate root (lib.rs or main.rs) or properly gated behind the correct feature flag
rules.fix_hint: Add `mod <module_name>;` to lib.rs/main.rs or check that the module path and feature flags are correct

rules.rule: When using edit_file, always use spaces (never tabs) in old_text to match file indentation, or read the file first to copy exact whitespace
rules.match_pattern: old_text not found.*space indentation|remove \\t from old_text
rules.fix_hint: Replace all tab characters in old_text with the correct number of spaces before calling edit_file

rules.rule: When using edit_file, include enough surrounding context lines (3+ lines or nearby unique identifiers) so old_text matches exactly one location in the file
rules.match_pattern: old_text found \d+ times in file|must be unique.*Add.*context
rules.fix_hint: Add 1-3 lines of surrounding context from read_file to disambiguate, or set replace_all=true if replacing every occurrence is intended

rules.rule: Verify package feature names exist before referencing them in pubspec.yaml or build commands — check the package's documentation or source for available features
rules.match_pattern: the package '[\w-]+' does not contain this feature:?
rules.fix_hint: Check the package's pubspec.yaml or README for the correct feature names and remove or correct invalid feature references

rules.rule: Always provide a valid non-empty file_path when calling edit_file; verify the path exists before editing
rules.match_pattern: cannot read file: open\s*: no such file or directory
rules.fix_hint: Ensure file_path is set to a valid, non-empty path that exists on disk before calling edit_file

rules.rule: Never pass natural language text, UI element names, or descriptions as file path arguments to git commands — only use actual file paths
rules.match_pattern: pathspec '.*' did not match any file\(s\) known to git
rules.tool_pattern: git add|git checkout|git rm|git mv|git restore
rules.fix_hint: Check that all pathspec arguments are valid file paths, not natural language text or descriptions

rules.rule: Never use `||` (logical OR) operators in Rust let chain conditions (e.g., `if let ... || ...`); only `&&` is supported — restructure with nested if-let, match, or separate conditions
rules.match_pattern: `\|\|` operators are not supported in let chain conditions
rules.fix_hint: Replace `if let X = y || cond` with nested `if let` blocks, a `match`, or extract the `||` condition into a separate bool variable checked before the let-chain

rules.rule: Ensure all single quotes in shell commands are balanced and properly escaped before executing; an odd number of unescaped quotes will cause the shell to fail with a syntax error
rules.match_pattern: unexpected EOF while looking for matching|syntax error: unexpected end of file
rules.fix_hint: Count single quotes in the command string; if odd, wrap the problematic segment in double quotes or escape embedded single quotes with '\''

rules.rule: Before using `use` statements for external crates or local workspace crates, verify the crate is listed in Cargo.toml dependencies (or dev-dependencies) and the correct feature flags are enabled
rules.fix_hint: Add the missing crate to Cargo.toml [dependencies] or enable the required feature flag, e.g. add `objc2 = "x.y"` or `raw-window-handle = "x.y"` to Cargo.toml

rules.rule: When a Rust crate supports multiple targets (terminal and desktop), gate desktop-only dependencies and their usages behind the correct feature flag — do not reference desktop dependency APIs in files compiled without that feature
rules.fix_hint: Wrap desktop-only imports/code in #[cfg(feature = "desktop")] (or the project's equivalent feature flag) and ensure the module is not compiled without it

rules.rule: When using multi_file_write, always provide each file's content as a plain string, never as an array or list
rules.match_pattern: multi_file_write.*cannot unmarshal array.*files\.content.*type string|json: cannot unmarshal array into.*files\.content
rules.fix_hint: Ensure each file's content field is a string (e.g. "line1\nline2"), not an array of strings

rules.rule: Always read_file to get exact current content before edit_file; never rely on memory or prior reads if the file may have changed
rules.fix_hint: Call read_file on the target file, then copy the exact text (including whitespace and newlines) into old_text
